findings.title: unsafe block without SAFETY comment
findings.description: Unsafe blocks should document why they're safe. Add a `// SAFETY:` comment.
findings.why_it_matters: Unsafe code bypasses Rust's safety guarantees. SAFETY comments are essential for code review.

findings.title: Single point of failure: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/walk.rs::impl<WorkerState>::scan:627` dominates 35 functions (11%)
findings.description: `/tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/walk.rs::impl<WorkerState>::scan:627` dominates 35 of 319 functions (11.0%). PageRank percentile: 0%. Blast radius boundary: /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/cli.rs::impl<Opts>::max_depth:724, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/cli.rs::impl<Opts>::threads:732, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/dir_entry.rs::impl<Colorable for DirEntry>::file_name:132, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/dir_entry.rs::impl<Colorable for DirEntry>::file_type:148, /tmp/nix-shell-540336-2497635915/tmp.zZnau8nCIx/fd/src/dir_entry.rs::impl<Colorable for DirEntry>::metadata:152 (+11 more)
findings.suggested_fix: Consider splitting this function into smaller, independently callable units. Extract sub-functionality behind interfaces so callers have alternative paths.
findings.why_it_matters: A single point of failure means all dependent code paths funnel through one function. If this function has a bug, performance issue, or API change, the blast radius is disproportionately large.

findings.title: Panic risk: unwrap() without context
findings.description: Using `unwrap()` can cause panics. Consider `?`, `unwrap_or`, or proper error handling.
findings.why_it_matters: Panics crash the program without recovery. Using proper error handling makes code more robust and debuggable.

findings.title: Circular dependency: 4 files
findings.description:
Mutual dependency between: config.rs ↔ dir_entry.rs ↔ filesystem.rs ↔ filetypes.rs

**Weakest link to break:** `config.rs` → `dir_entry.rs` (1 import)
findings.suggested_fix:
🔗 **Best place to break:** `config.rs` → `dir_entry.rs` (weakest coupling: 1 imports)

Since `config.rs` only imports 1 symbol(s) from `dir_entry.rs`, consider:
- Move those symbols to a shared module
- Pass them as parameters instead of importing
- Use dependency injection

**For small cycles:**
1. Merge tightly coupled modules if they're always used together
2. Use TYPE_CHECKING (Python) or type-only imports (TS) for type hints
3. Extract common code to a third module
findings.why_it_matters: Circular dependencies make code harder to understand, test, and maintain. They can cause import errors at runtime and make it difficult to refactor individual modules.

findings.title: Mutex poisoning risk
findings.description: [COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Using .lock().unwrap() will panic if the mutex is poisoned. Consider handling the PoisonError or using parking_lot::Mutex.
findings.suggested_fix:
Handle poisoning: `mutex.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())`
Or use `parking_lot::Mutex` which has no poisoning.
findings.why_it_matters: Poisoned mutexes cause cascading panics across threads.

findings.title: Long method: spawn_senders (181 lines)
findings.description:
[COMPOUND: 2 co-located issues] Function 'spawn_senders' has 181 lines (effective threshold: 150).

**Graph Analysis:**
Orchestrator pattern: calls 17 functions (reduced severity)
Calls 3 different modules - possible split points
findings.suggested_fix:
This appears to be an orchestrator function (coordinates many calls).
If it must remain long, ensure it:
1. Has clear section comments
2. Handles errors at each step
3. Has a clear flow (consider a state machine for complex flows)
findings.why_it_matters: Long methods are hard to understand, test, and maintain. Each function should do one thing well.
